title: Local review keeps workspace-repo file references out of machine-local links

provenance.originSummary: Synthetic benchmark scenario designed to exercise the rule directly.
provenance.whyItMattered: Use this case to benchmark local-review rendering of workspace-repo-relative file references and to prevent machine-local link leakage in the final review body.
provenance.genericCondition: Synthetic fixture models a local-review run where the historical failure mode was rendering workspace files as editor-session or absolute-disk links instead of workspace-repo-relative references.

description: A sanitized local-review scenario where AI-customization files change and the final rendered review should use workspace-repo-relative file references rather than `vscode-file://`, `file:///`, or absolute machine-local disk paths.

scope.notes: Synthetic fixture models a local-review run where the modeled failure mode was rendering workspace files as editor-session or absolute-disk links instead of workspace-repo-relative references.

mustCatch.description: The local review should preserve workspace-repo-relative file references in the rendered review body instead of converting them into machine-local URIs or absolute disk paths.
The final local review body should not contain `vscode-file://`, `file:///`, `C:\`, `/Users/`, `AppData`, or `workspaceStorage` fragments anywhere in the rendered output.

mustNotFlag.description: The local review must not emit absolute machine-local disk paths or URIs in file lists, findings, or standards-check bullets.

notes: Use this case to benchmark local-review rendering of workspace-repo-relative file references and to prevent machine-local link leakage in the final review body.
